sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Amended deal leads AMD to acquire $2.1 billion worth of wafers from GlobalFoundries
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has amended its agreement with GlobalFoundries, increasing its commitment to acquire $2.1 billion worth of silicon wafers from 2022 to 2025. This updated deal, following a regulatory filing, supersedes a previous agreement for $1.6 billion in chips. While Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) is AMD's primary supplier for 'chiplets', GlobalFoundries will continue to provide critical components for assembling AMD's processors.

feed.title: Semiconductor material leader applies for global market share as high as 30% Huawei Hubble, medium and micro holding shares
feed.summary: Tianyue Advanced, a leading domestic semi-insulating substrate manufacturer, is applying for a listing. The company held a 30% global market share by the end of 2020 and its gross profit margin has surpassed foreign competitors. Huawei Hubble owns about 7% of the company, and China Micro owns about 0.99%.

feed.title: AMD Unveils New Power-Efficient, High-Performance Mobile Graphics for Premium and Thin-and-Light Laptops, and New Desktop Graphics Cards
feed.summary: AMD has announced new additions and enhancements to its Radeon graphics

feed.title: AMD Presents Latest High-Performance Computing Technologies in 2022 Product Premiere Livestream
feed.summary: AMD announced over 30 new products in its 2022 Product Premiere livestream, including Ryzen 6000 Series processors for notebooks, new Radeon RX 6000 Series GPUs for laptops and desktops, and the Ryzen 7 5800X3D desktop processor. The company also previewed its forthcoming Ryzen 7000 Series desktop processors based on the new "Zen 4" architecture. These releases aim to deliver leadership performance for productivity, content creation, and gaming experiences across various PC segments.

feed.title: AMD Unveils New Ryzen Mobile Processors Uniting “Zen 3+” core with AMD RDNA 2 Graphics in Powerhouse Design
feed.summary: AMD has announced its new Ryzen 6000 Series processors for laptops, combining the "Zen 3+" core architecture with RDNA 2 graphics for enhanced performance and battery life. Additionally, AMD introduced the Ryzen 7 5800X3D desktop processor with 3D V-Cache technology for superior gaming and preview previewed the upcoming Ryzen 7000 Series with "Zen 4" architecture. These announcements highlight AMD's focus on innovation across mobile and desktop computing, emphasizing efficiency, graphics capabilities, and security features.

feed.title: Qualcomm, Microsoft partner on metaverse chip for AR glasses
feed.summary: Qualcomm and Microsoft are collaborating on custom chips for lightweight augmented reality (AR) glasses, targeting both consumers and businesses for metaverse applications. The partnership aims to integrate these chips with Microsoft's Mesh software for virtual presence and Qualcomm's Snapdragon Spaces for AR functions like spatial mapping and hand-tracking. Though no release date was provided, the initiative seeks to advance wearable AR devices for broader adoption.

feed.title: Qualcomm unveils ‘digital chassis’ tech at CES as it diversifies beyond smartphones
feed.summary: Qualcomm introduced its "digital chassis" strategy at CES, aiming to expand its mobile technology expertise beyond smartphones and into the automotive industry. This includes a new Snapdragon Ride Vision System for advanced driver assistance and infotainment deals with major car manufacturers like Volvo, Honda, and Renault. The company projects its automotive revenue to reach $3.5 billion annually in five years, signaling a significant diversification effort.

feed.title: HP OMEN reveals products for 2022, including the unique cooling device, the OMEN Cryo Chamber
feed.summary: HP OMEN has unveiled its 2022 gaming lineup, featuring the OMEN 45L Desktop with the innovative OMEN Cryo Chamber CPU cooling solution, which significantly lowers temperatures. The lineup also includes the OMEN 25L Desktop, the compact Victus by HP 15L Desktop, and the OMEN 27u 4K Gaming Monitor, designed for next-gen consoles. These new products offer advanced performance, cooling, and customization options for a diverse gaming audience.

feed.title: The ‘Bividend’: What’s Up With BTCS’s Bitcoin Dividend?
feed.summary: BTCS, a blockchain technology company, announced the first-ever dividend payable in bitcoin by a Nasdaq-listed firm, dubbed a "bividend." While some critics view it as a marketing stunt due to Bitcoin's speculative nature and the dividend's one-time status, the move significantly boosted BTCS's stock price. The article suggests that despite the controversy, other companies might follow suit, adopting similar crypto dividends as an attention-getting strategy.

feed.title: CES 2022: HP shows off Elite Dragonfly G3, Dragonfly Chromebook and lots more new hardware
feed.summary: At CES 2022, HP unveiled a comprehensive portfolio targeting the hybrid-work market, including the Elite Dragonfly G3, Dragonfly Chromebook, and several new monitors, laptops, and desktop PCs. The new Elite Dragonfly G3 is a premium ultraportable laptop with a 13.5-inch screen and 12th-gen Intel Core processors, while the Dragonfly Chromebook offers a Chrome OS alternative with unique features like a haptic trackpad. HP also introduced various conferencing and webcam monitors, updated EliteBook and ProBook business laptops, and new desktop PCs and curved displays for creators.

feed.title: Ambarella unveils AI chips for autonomous vehicles at CES 2022
feed.summary: Ambarella announced its new CV3 AI domain controller family at CES 2022, designed to provide high AI processing performance for autonomous vehicles. These single-chip components enable centralized, multi-sensor perception and AV path planning, supporting systems from ADAS to Level 4 AVs. The CV3 family offers significant performance boosts and hardware scalability, allowing automakers to unify software stacks across their vehicle portfolios.

feed.title: Nvidia buys high-performance computing cluster management software provider Bright Computing
feed.summary: Nvidia has acquired Bright Computing Inc., a company specializing in software for managing high-performance computing (HPC) systems. This acquisition aims to enhance Nvidia's offerings in the HPC market, integrating Bright's Cluster Manager into Nvidia's software stack to provide more complete infrastructure platforms for accelerated computing. The move is seen as another step in Nvidia's evolution from a chip company to a comprehensive solution provider for data centers, particularly as HPC and AI workloads converge.

feed.title: Hesai Technology showcased new automotive-grade hybrid solid-state Lidar AT128
feed.summary: Hesai Technology showcased its new automotive-grade hybrid solid-state lidar AT128 at CES 2022. This long-range sensor is designed for L3+ ADAS applications in mass production vehicles, featuring image-level resolution, superior ranging capability, and automotive-grade reliability, with mass production slated for 2022. Hesai also unveiled the QT128, a short-range lidar aimed at L4 applications, entering mass production in Q1 2023.

feed.title: Maze Therapeutics Nets $190M to Propel Precision Medicine Programs
feed.summary: Maze Therapeutics secured $190 million in financing to advance its precision medicine pipeline, which includes nine programs. The company, founded in 2019, focuses on developing treatments for diseases like Pompe disease, Phenylketonuria, ALS, and chronic kidney disease, leveraging insights from human genetics. Matrix Capital Management led the new investment round, and Andy Tran from Matrix Capital Management joined Maze's board.

feed.title: Stellar December gives TSMC record year
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) reported record sales for the previous year, driven by strong demand for its advanced 5-nanometer process used in emerging sectors like 5G and high-performance computing. The company's consolidated sales reached NT$1.59 trillion ($57.47 billion USD), an 18.5 percent increase from 2020. TSMC is expected to announce a significant capital expenditure budget for the current year to further expand production capacity and maintain its technological advantage.

feed.title: Corsair One i300 gaming PC review
feed.summary: The Corsair One i300 is a compact, high-end gaming PC featuring Intel's Core i9 12900K, an Nvidia GeForce RTX 3080 Ti, and 64GB of DDR5-4800 RAM, priced at $4,999. It delivers excellent 4K gaming performance and operates quietly due to its unique liquid cooling system, though the CPU can run hot under heavy load. The article praises its aesthetic and hardware-packed design, acknowledging its high cost but highlighting its cutting-edge components and quiet operation.

feed.title: Why AMD May Be Worth Its Expensive Valuation
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) recently launched over 30 new products and has upcoming earnings later this month. Barclays hiked its price target for AMD to $148, boosting the stock, which is up over 41% year-over-year. Despite a high valuation, AMD has shown significant revenue and net income growth, coupled with a strong balance sheet, suggesting long-term potential.

feed.title: Comcast Announces World-First Test of 10G Modem Technology Capable of Delivering Multigigabit Speeds to Homes
feed.summary: Comcast successfully tested a prototype 10G modem using Full Duplex DOCSIS 4.0 technology, achieving upload and download speeds faster than 4 Gbps. This marks a significant milestone in Comcast's 10G initiative, demonstrating the capability to deliver multigigabit speeds over existing network connections and promising increased reliability, performance, and lower latency for customers.

feed.title: 投资指南：2022年的5只精选区块链ETF
feed.summary: 这篇文章介绍了2022年值得关注的五只精选区块链ETF，为普通投资者提供了投资区块链和加密货币类上市公司的途径。文章详细分析了每只ETF的资产规模、持股重点、费用率以及成立以来的业绩表现，旨在帮助投资者根据自身需求选择合适的区块链投资产品。

feed.title: What will be included in 5G Advanced Release 18?
feed.summary: The upcoming 5G Advanced Release 18 will focus on end-to-end system enhancements and expanding support for new device types and use cases. Qualcomm's Juan Montojo detailed improvements such as MIMO radio arrays, mobile Integrated Access/Backhaul, AI/ML-driven network design, enhanced mobility, and optimizing power consumption. The release also aims to develop a scalable, unified air interface to support a diverse range of devices, from high-performance XR headsets to various IoT sensors, paving the way for the future 6G vision.

feed.title: These 3 ETFs let you play the hot semiconductor sector, where Nvidia, Micron, AMD and others are growing sales rapidly
feed.summary: This article highlights three ETFs that offer investors exposure to the rapidly growing semiconductor sector, featuring companies like Nvidia, Micron, and AMD. The chip-focused exchange-traded funds have significantly outperformed the S&P 500 and Nasdaq over the past 15 years, indicating their strong performance potential despite market pressures. The article also mentions Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSM) reporting robust fourth-quarter sales and earnings, reinforcing the sector's positive outlook.

feed.title: These 3 ETFs let you play the hot semiconductor sector, where Nvidia, Micron, AMD and others are growing sales rapidly
feed.summary: The article suggests that the semiconductor sector remains a strong investment area, even with rising interest rates impacting other tech stocks. It highlights the strong performance of chip exchange-traded funds (ETFs) over the past 15 years, outperforming both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq. This positive outlook is further supported by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s impressive fourth-quarter results and significant capital expenditure plans for 2022.

feed.title: These 3 ETFs let you play the hot semiconductor sector, where Nvidia, Micron, AMD and others are growing sales rapidly
feed.summary: This article discusses the continued growth potential of the semiconductor sector and how investors can capitalize on it through ETFs. Despite concerns about rising interest rates affecting tech stocks, chipmakers are demonstrating strong sales growth. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSM) reported significant increases in its fourth-quarter sales and earnings, signaling a robust outlook for the industry.

feed.title: These 3 ETFs let you play the hot semiconductor sector, where Nvidia, Micron, AMD and others are growing sales rapidly
feed.summary: This article highlights three ETFs that allow investors to gain exposure to the rapidly growing semiconductor sector, which includes companies like Nvidia, Micron, and AMD. Despite concerns about rising interest rates, the semiconductor industry continues to show strong growth, as evidenced by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s impressive Q4 sales and earnings report. The chip-focused exchange-traded funds have significantly outperformed both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq over the past 15 years.

feed.title: These 3 ETFs let you play the hot semiconductor sector, where Nvidia, Micron, AMD and others are growing sales rapidly
feed.summary: This article discusses the semiconductor sector as a potentially excellent area for investors, even amidst rising interest rates. It highlights how chip exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have significantly outperformed major benchmarks. The sector's growth is exemplified by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s strong fourth-quarter results and substantial capital expenditure plans for 2022.

feed.title: These 3 ETFs let you play the hot semiconductor sector, where Nvidia, Micron, AMD and others are growing sales rapidly
feed.summary: This article suggests that semiconductor stocks remain an excellent investment area despite rising interest rates, highlighting the robust sales growth of companies like Nvidia, Micron, and AMD. It points to three ETFs that allow investors to capitalize on this hot sector, which has significantly outperformed the S&P 500 and Nasdaq over the past 15 years. The positive outlook was reinforced by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.'s strong Q4 sales and earnings report and substantial planned capital expenditures for 2022.

feed.title: Intel reportedly picking Ohio for factory that also had a chance at landing in Central Texas
feed.summary: Intel is reportedly planning to build a new semiconductor plant in Ohio, bypassing Central Texas, which was also considered for the factory. This move aligns with CEO Pat Gelsinger's goal to establish Intel's role in a national semiconductor strategy, with plans for significant investment in new fabrication facilities. An official announcement is anticipated around January 21st.

feed.title: The Money Printing Press That Is Chip Maker TSMC
feed.summary: TSMC, the world's largest chip etcher, continues to be a highly profitable enterprise, consistently investing ahead of demand and achieving strong financial results. The company's capital expenditures are a leading indicator for future revenues, and it anticipates continued growth driven by high-performance computing (HPC) and 5G applications. TSMC is also advancing its manufacturing processes with new variants of its 5 nanometer node and the upcoming 3 nanometer node, with significant investments planned for these advanced technologies.

feed.title: Initial Sound Open Firmware Support For AMD Hardware Comes With Linux 5.17
feed.summary: The Linux 5.17 kernel updates include initial Sound Open Firmware (SOF) support for AMD hardware, specifically the Renoir Audio Co-Processor. This marks a significant step for AMD, bringing its audio hardware into the open-source SOF ecosystem previously championed by Intel. The update also adds HDA support for new audio codecs and other ASoC improvements.

feed.title: AMD’s Ryzen CPU Reportedly Cuts Down Tesla Model 3’s Driving Range
feed.summary: A report from Teslascope suggests that the AMD Ryzen microprocessors, recently installed in Tesla Model 3 vehicles, are causing a reduction in their driving range. Tesla is reportedly asking customers who pre-ordered a Model 3 to accept a range decrease, particularly in jurisdictions with strong consumer protection laws. While Tesla’s message attributes this to a new, power-intensive computer to optimize the touchscreen experience, the Model Y's range appears to have increased due to larger batteries, contrasting with the Model 3's reduction.

feed.title: Intel places ASML order to step up TSMC competition
feed.summary: Intel has ordered ASML's new TWINSCAN EXE:5200 extreme ultraviolet (EUV) system, marking a move to compete with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) in advancing to 2-nanometer process technology. This purchase aims to accelerate Intel's production with the new technology by 2025. While Intel is the first to order, a supply chain source suggests TSMC is also likely to acquire the system and lead in commercializing 2-nanometer production.

feed.title: Intel’s curious revelation of its ASML deal raises questions
feed.summary: Intel's recent announcement to purchase advanced chipmaking tools from ASML has drawn scrutiny from analysts, who suggest it's a strategic move to address market doubts about its manufacturing capabilities. This acquisition of ASML’s next-generation EUV lithography machine is seen as Intel's effort to develop more advanced 2 nm chips, challenging leaders like TSMC and Samsung in the foundry market. The unusual public disclosure of such an industry deal aims to bolster confidence in Intel's renewed commitment to contract chip manufacturing.

feed.title: Intel's $20 bln Ohio factory could become world's largest chip plant
feed.summary: Intel announced plans to invest an initial $20 billion in a new chip manufacturing complex in Ohio, which could expand to $100 billion with eight fabrication plants, potentially making it the world's largest. This move is part of CEO Pat Gelsinger's strategy to restore Intel's chipmaking dominance and reduce U.S. reliance on Asian manufacturing amidst a global semiconductor shortage. The company is actively seeking government subsidies and expects the shortage to last into 2023.

feed.title: Low-power AI computer chip startup Ceremorphic exits stealth with $50M funding
feed.summary: Ceremorphic Inc., a startup focused on energy-efficient AI supercomputing chips, has emerged from stealth mode with a $50 million Series A funding round. The company, founded by tech veteran Dr. Venkat Mattela, aims to develop a "complete silicon system" incorporating a 5-nanometer processor with a unique architecture and patented multithread technology (ThreadArch) to address reliability, security, and energy consumption challenges in high-performance AI, HPC, and metaverse applications. The funding for the Series A round was entirely provided by the company's founders, many of whom have a background with Redpine Signals Inc., known for ultralow-power wireless chipsets.

feed.title: Silicon Labs Integrates AI/ML Acceleration in Low-power Wireless SoCs
feed.summary: Silicon Labs has released two new families of wireless SoCs, BG24 and MG24, that integrate AI/ML accelerators for low-power IoT edge processing. These SoCs support a wide range of protocols and offer significant power reduction and speed increase for AI/ML applications. The company also introduced a software platform to aid developers in deploying AI/ML algorithms for various use cases, including smart home applications and medical devices.

feed.title: Lenovo Launches HPC As-A-Service To Fight Dell Apex, HPE GreenLake
feed.summary: Lenovo has launched a new High-Performance Computing (HPC) as-a-service offering as part of its TruScale portfolio, directly competing with Dell Technologies' Apex and HPE GreenLake. This service provides on-premises supercomputing capabilities with a pay-per-use model, managed by Lenovo and its channel partners. The move aligns with Lenovo's "One Lenovo" vision to expand beyond PCs into a comprehensive IT solutions provider and aims to reach $100 billion in revenue by taking market share from rivals.

feed.title: Intel scores major win as court scraps $1.2 bln EU antitrust fine
feed.summary: Intel has successfully overturned a 1.06-billion-euro ($1.2 billion) EU antitrust fine that was imposed 12 years ago for allegedly stifling rival Advanced Micro Devices. Europe's second-highest court annulled the fine, criticizing the European Commission's analysis as incomplete. This ruling is a significant setback for EU antitrust regulators and could impact similar cases involving other major tech companies.

feed.title: CRDO Stock Price, Forecast & Analysis | CREDO TECHNOLOGY GROUP HOLDI (NASDAQ:CRDO)
feed.summary: The article provides a detailed analysis of CREDO Technology Group Holding (NASDAQ:CRDO), covering its stock price, forecast, and various analytical ratings. It highlights CRDO's strong performance in the past year, its technical and fundamental ratings from ChartMill, and analysts' expectations for significant future growth in both EPS and revenue. The company specializes in high-speed connectivity solutions for the data infrastructure market.

feed.title: Western Digital Taps Semiconductor Veteran for CFO Job
feed.summary: Western Digital has appointed Wissam Jabre, a semiconductor industry veteran, as its new Chief Financial Officer. This move aligns with the hard-drive manufacturer's strategy to expand its memory-chip business. Mr. Jabre previously served as CFO at Dialog Semiconductor PLC and held finance roles at Advanced Micro Devices Inc. and Freescale Semiconductor Inc.

feed.title: Corsair One i300 Review: Power Packed In
feed.summary: The Corsair One i300 is a compact gaming PC that offers powerful performance with high-end components like an Intel Core i9-12900K and Nvidia GeForce RTX 3080 Ti in a 12-liter chassis. While it provides big performance in a small package, making it ideal for limited desk space, its small size sacrifices some upgradeability and comes at a premium price, starting at $3,799.99 and reaching $4,999.99 as tested. The review highlights its strong gaming and productivity performance, outperforming larger systems in some benchmarks.

feed.title: Chip shortage to last six months, one year or 18 months-- depending on who’s talking
feed.summary: The article discusses the varying predictions for how long the global chip shortage will last, with estimates ranging from six months to 18 months or longer. It highlights views from IDC, Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, and Texas Instruments, alongside ongoing efforts by the U.S. Congress and Commerce Secretary to address the issue through funding and incentives for domestic chip production. The consensus indicates that while some improvements might be seen, the shortage is expected to persist through 2022 and potentially into 2023 for various sectors.

feed.title: Reference design offers wireless docking over Wi-Fi 6E for PCs
feed.summary: Synaptics Incorporated has introduced Gemini, a reference design that offers wireless docking over Wi-Fi 6E for PCs, claiming it's the world's first true Wi-Fi 6/6E wireless docking experience for laptops. This technology enables full dual-4K display capability and ultra-low latency, comparable to wired connections, and integrates hardware and software components like the BCM43752 Wi-Fi 6/6E radio and DL-1950 video interface IC. While initially focused on PC docking, Gemini's applications extend to tablets, gaming, and VR, with analyst Patrick Moorhead highlighting its potential to revolutionize connectivity in hybrid work environments.

feed.title: Cadence Platform Accelerates Microchip’s Data Center Solutions SoC Development
feed.summary: Microchip Technology Inc. has adopted Cadence's Palladium Z2 Enterprise Emulation Platform for the development of its next-generation ASIC products, specifically targeting high-performance and scalable SoC solutions for data centers. The Palladium Z2 platform offers Microchip 2X better emulation capacity and 1.5X greater performance, alongside seamless integration with Cadence's Protium Enterprise Prototyping system. This adoption significantly accelerates Microchip's time-to-market and quality requirements for complex multi-chip systems.

feed.title: Infographic: Highlights of Advanced Micro Devices’ (AMD) Q4 results
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) reported strong fourth-quarter 2021 earnings, with adjusted earnings per share rising to $0.92 from $0.52 year-over-year, exceeding analyst estimates. Total revenues increased by 49% annually to $4.8 billion, also surpassing Wall Street's predictions. The chipmaker saw its net income reach $974 million or $0.80 per share for the quarter.

feed.title: ETF Flows Cool Off as Stocks Stumble Out of the Gate
feed.summary: January 2022 saw a significant cool-off in ETF flows and a stumble in stock markets globally, marking the worst monthly showing for the Morningstar Global Markets Index since March 2020. Fixed-income ETFs also experienced outflows, their first since March 2020, as investors reallocated their money amidst interest rate hike predictions and a strong performance from the energy sector. Vanguard dominated ETF provider flows, while drug and digital-asset focused ETFs appeared cheap, reflecting market turbulence and potential undervaluation.

feed.title: AMD's stellar report fuels chip stock rally
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) reported strong quarterly demand for its semiconductors, boosting its annual revenue forecast and surpassing analysts' expectations, which led to an almost 11% surge in its stock. This positive report ignited a rally in other chip stocks, with Nvidia, Qualcomm, and Micron Technology also seeing jumps in their share prices, despite ongoing global supply snags and volatility in the semiconductor sector. Intel, however, saw a decline in its stock.

feed.title: Ansys free student downloads: Master simulation skills to solve real-life problems
feed.summary: Ansys offers free student downloads of its engineering simulation software to help aspiring engineers develop practical skills for real-world problems. The article highlights five key Ansys tools: Ansys Student, Ansys Discovery Student, Ansys LS-DYNA, Ansys Electronics Desktop Student, and SCADE Student, each designed for different aspects of simulation and analysis. These tools enable students to gain experience in fields like fluid dynamics, structural mechanics, electromagnetics, and model-based design, preparing them for careers in various industries.

feed.title: Alphabet eyes $2 trillion value after blowout results
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc. is on the verge of joining Apple and Microsoft in the $2 trillion market valuation club after a strong quarterly report led to an over 8% surge in its shares. The company also announced a 20-to-1 stock split, aiming to make shares more accessible to retail investors. At least 20 brokerages raised their price targets on Alphabet's stock.

feed.title: Dying Light 2 Stay Human Gaming PC Built by Newegg Available Through Intel Sweepstakes
feed.summary: Newegg is collaborating with Intel on a sweepstakes to give away a custom-built, Dying Light 2 Stay Human-themed gaming PC, coinciding with the game's release. The unique PC, featuring an Intel Core i9-12900K processor and valued at approximately $5,000, includes game-themed visual elements. Additionally, qualifying purchases of 12th Gen Intel Core-powered devices from Newegg will receive a free digital download of the game starting February 19.

feed.title: Chip Startup Ceremorphic Seeks AI, HPC Edge With New 5nm Design
feed.summary: Ceremorphic, a chip startup, has emerged from stealth mode with plans to produce a scalable, low-energy processor for AI and high-performance computing using TSMC's 5-nanometer process node. The company's CEO, Venkat Mattela, emphasizes that access to this advanced manufacturing process, secured through his team's prior success, as well as Ceremorphic's proprietary technologies for efficiency, reliability, and security, will set its Hierarchical Learning Processor apart from competitors. The startup aims to deliver samples to customers in 2023 and begin production in 2024, following test chip validation in March.

feed.title: Texas Instruments plans to invest billions in semiconductor chips to combat shortage
feed.summary: Texas Instruments plans to invest $3.5 billion annually through 2025, and then 10% of annual revenue from 2026-2030, to address the global semiconductor chip shortage. These chips are crucial for car and cell phone production, and demand has increased significantly since 2019. The Lake Highlands-based company will build new factories, primarily in Sherman, Texas, to meet this demand, as auto sales alone accounted for 21% of their sales this year.

feed.title: SoftBank’s sale of Arm to Nvidia collapses, Arm to IPO
feed.summary: The proposed sale of Arm Ltd by SoftBank Group Corp to U.S. chipmaker Nvidia Corp has collapsed due to regulatory hurdles and scrutiny from various international bodies. Instead of the sale, which was valued at up to $80 billion, Arm will now plan for an initial public offering (IPO). The deal faced opposition from U.S., British, EU, and Chinese regulators over concerns about competition, pricing, and innovation in vital chip markets.

feed.title: What Nvidia Can’t Buy, It Can Still Get Through An Arm Partnership
feed.summary: Nvidia's $40 billion acquisition of Arm Holdings from SoftBank Group was terminated due to regulatory scrutiny and market resistance, resulting in a $1.25 billion hit for Nvidia. Despite the failed acquisition, Nvidia, an Arm architecture licensee, plans to release its Arm server chip "Grace" next year and may pursue a joint venture partnership with Arm, allowing it to license its GPU and network chip technologies through Arm's licensing machine. The article suggests that such a partnership could enable many of the benefits Nvidia sought through ownership without the regulatory hurdles.

feed.title: Design Challenges Increasing For Mixed-Die Packages
feed.summary: The semiconductor industry faces growing design challenges for mixed-die packages as it moves beyond Moore's Law towards heterogeneous integration. While combining dies from different foundries offers benefits like cost reduction and optimal IP use, it also introduces complexities in supply chain relationships, liability, and the need for standardized interconnect schemes. The article highlights that business and organizational hurdles may be more significant than technological ones in advancing chiplet adoption, requiring closer collaboration across the ecosystem.

feed.title: AMD closes landmark $50B acquisition of chipmaker Xilinx
feed.summary: AMD has finalized its $50 billion acquisition of Xilinx, an all-stock deal initially valued at $35 billion in 2020. This acquisition transforms Xilinx into AMD's new Adaptive and Embedded Computing Group, aiming to expand AMD's presence in the data center, 5G, and other markets with Xilinx's leading field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs) and adaptive SoCs. The merger enhances AMD's portfolio of high-performance computing solutions and increases its total addressable market.

feed.title: Lisa Su named AMD chair after completing $49B acquisition of Xilinx
feed.summary: Lisa Su has been named the chair of AMD following the completion of its $49 billion acquisition of Xilinx. This strategic move expands AMD's market focus, with the company estimating the value of its newly targeted markets to exceed $135 billion. The acquisition and promotion of Su highlight AMD's aggressive growth strategy in the semiconductor industry.

feed.title: AMD Completes $49B Xilinx Acquisition, Largest Chip Deal In History
feed.summary: AMD has finalized its $49 billion acquisition of Xilinx, making it the largest chip deal in history and transforming AMD into an "industry’s high-performance and adaptive computing leader." This strategic move expands AMD's offerings beyond CPUs and GPUs into reprogrammable FPGAs, significantly boosting its presence in data centers, embedded computing, and telecommunications. The deal will also enhance AMD's total addressable market and is expected to be accretive to margins, earnings per share, and free cash flow in the first year.

feed.title: AMD Closes Xilinx Deal: First Products Expected in 2023
feed.summary: AMD has completed its acquisition of Xilinx, a move expected to significantly expand AMD's presence in the datacenter sector by combining CPUs, GPUs, and FPGAs. The first processors integrating Xilinx technologies are anticipated in 2023, showcasing a rapid integration compared to AMD's previous acquisitions. This merger aims to create a high-performance and adaptive computing leader, offering a comprehensive portfolio across cloud, edge, and intelligent devices.

feed.title: AMD Closes Record Chip Industry Deal with about $50-Bln Purchase of Xilinx
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) has finalized its acquisition of Xilinx Inc. for approximately $50 billion, making it a record deal in the chip industry. This acquisition allows AMD to expand into the cloud, edge, and intelligent devices market, intensifying its competition with Intel Corp. in the data center chip sector. The combined entity will be led by AMD CEO Lisa Su and aims to leverage an outsourced manufacturing strategy, primarily with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd (TSMC).

feed.title: AMD Closes $50 Billion Xilinx Acquisition
feed.summary: AMD has completed its acquisition of Xilinx for approximately $50 billion, a deal initially valued at $35 billion in October 2020. This acquisition significantly boosts AMD's ability to compete with Intel in the data center chip sector by offering a comprehensive range of High Performance Computing (HPC) technologies, including CPUs, GPUs, FPGAs, and Adaptive SoCs. The former Xilinx business will operate as AMD's Adaptive and Embedded Computing Group (AECG), led by ex-Xilinx CEO Victor Peng.

feed.title: AMD's Market Cap Surpasses Intel for the First Time in History
feed.summary: AMD's market capitalization has surpassed Intel's for the first time in history, reaching approximately $197.75 billion compared to Intel's $197.24 billion as of February 15. This surge is primarily attributed to AMD's recent $49 billion acquisition of Xilinx, which significantly increased AMD's share count. While Intel remains a larger company by revenue and overall x86 market share, AMD's higher valuation reflects greater market optimism about its future growth prospects and the strategic advantages gained from the Xilinx acquisition.

feed.title: Intel Buying Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 Billion to Boost Supply Chain Capacity
feed.summary: Intel is acquiring Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 billion to expand its manufacturing capacity and strengthen its Intel Foundry Services (IFS) division. This acquisition is a key part of Intel's IDM 2.0 strategy, aiming to boost supply chain resilience and compete with leading chip manufacturers like TSMC. Tower Semiconductor will provide Intel with access to high-growth markets and enhance the scale of its foundry operations, which Intel believes is crucial for accelerating the growth of IFS.

feed.title: Tower Semi deal won’t solve Intel’s margin issues, analysts say
feed.summary: Intel's planned acquisition of Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 billion is unlikely to resolve its ongoing gross margin issues, according to analysts. While the deal aims to expand Intel's manufacturing capacity with new foundries, analysts suggest these additions focus on lower-margin production and will not help Intel become a leader in cutting-edge foundry technology. Wall Street has expressed dissatisfaction with Intel's declining gross margins, as CEO Pat Gelsinger invests in capacity building at the expense of immediate profitability.

feed.title: Intel to Buy Israel’s Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 Billion
feed.summary: Intel Corp. has agreed to acquire Israel’s Tower Semiconductor Ltd. for $5.4 billion as part of CEO Pat Gelsinger’s strategy to expand into the outsourced chip-manufacturing business. This acquisition aims to enhance Intel's expertise and customer base in the chip-foundry market, directly competing with industry leaders like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. The deal is expected to close in about 12 months and is a key part of Intel's broader investment cycle to modernize its factories and rebuild manufacturing capabilities.

feed.title: AMD Radeon ProRender for Rhino
feed.summary: AMD has released an updated, free, physically-based renderer called Radeon ProRender for Rhino, which is deeply integrated into Rhino 7.0. This renderer can utilize either the CPU or GPU, with GPU acceleration offering faster performance, especially with AMD Radeon PRO W6000 series GPUs featuring hardware raytracing. It allows users to render photorealistic imagery directly within the Rhino viewport, supports existing Rhino material definitions, and offers flexible lighting and scene setup options.

feed.title: Intel to buy Israel's Tower Semiconductor for $5.4bn
feed.summary: Intel announced its acquisition of Israeli chipmaker Tower Semiconductor for $5.4 billion. This deal strengthens Intel's position in specialized chip production, particularly for analog chips used in various applications like automotive and medical sensors. The acquisition aims to capitalize on the increasing global demand for semiconductors.

feed.title: Nvidia Posts Record Sales Amid Broad Chip Demand
feed.summary: Nvidia Corp. reported record quarterly sales of $7.64 billion, a 53% increase from the previous year, and projected further growth. The graphics-chip maker also more than doubled its net income to $3 billion, surpassing Wall Street expectations for both profit and sales. This strong performance comes amidst broad chip demand, despite the company moving past its failed takeover bid for Arm Ltd.

feed.title: Inside Pat Gelsinger’s audacious $43.5B plan to revive Intel
feed.summary: This article details Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger's plans to revitalize the company, which has recently faced significant challenges from competitors like Apple and AMD, and struggled with manufacturing delays. Gelsinger, who rejoined Intel after a period away, aims to tackle both technological and cultural issues to restore Intel's preeminence in the chip industry. His experience at Intel during its heyday positions him to address these intricate problems, including a new business model centered on investing $43.5 billion to regain its manufacturing edge.

feed.title: Intel delays 2023 server chip, says it needs to boost spending to catch competitors
feed.summary: Intel announced it would need to significantly increase spending over the next two years to fund its turnaround and transformation into a semiconductor manufacturer. The company also confirmed that the release of its forthcoming server chip, codenamed Granite Rapids, has been pushed from 2023 to 2024. This delay and investment strategy led to a drop in Intel's stock as investors questioned the impact on profit margins and the capital-intensive nature of its new foundry business.

feed.title: Intel "Tick Tock" Alive Again, Company Announces New Intel 18A Node (1.8 nm Class)
feed.summary: Intel is revitalizing its "tick-tock" product development cycle, announcing new fabrication nodes after a six-year hiatus. The company plans to introduce Intel 4 (7 nm EUV-based) with "Meteor Lake" in H1 2023, followed by Intel 3 in late 2023, and then enter the sub-2 nm class with Intel 20A in H1 2024 and Intel 18A later that year. This strategy involves decoupling development for server, client, and GPU segments, with "Arrow Lake" being developed on the Intel 20A node for 2024 launch.

feed.title: The Processor Chip Industry Is Another Ugly Capitalist Oligopoly
feed.summary: This article criticizes the semiconductor industry, labeling it as an "ugly capitalist oligopoly" due to the extreme concentration of chip manufacturing among a few companies, primarily Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC). It highlights how this concentration, driven by high capital costs for fabrication plants and state subsidies, leads to supply chain vulnerabilities, inflation, and limited competition. The author argues that even efforts by governments like the US to subsidize domestic chip production won't fundamentally alter the industry's oligopolistic structure or shift its profit-driven nature.

feed.title: AMD CPU Shipments in Japan Sink to 25% of Total, Intel Achieves 74%
feed.summary: AMD's CPU market share in Japan fell to 25% in January, a three-year low, while Intel captured 74% of total sales. This decline follows a period in 2020 where AMD reached market share highs of 68-70%, buoyed by its Ryzen 5 series and Intel's 10nm production issues. The recent shift is attributed to Intel's recovery from manufacturing woes and the successful launch of its Alder Lake processors, which restored performance parity with AMD.

feed.title: Like building the world's tallest tower every two years
feed.summary: This article delves into the critical role of Intel Israel's Core Group in developing the most essential parts of Intel's processors: the cores. It explains the evolution of processor cores from single-core to multi-core designs, highlighting the challenges and innovations, including the creation of hybrid architectures with Performance and Efficiency Cores in the Alder Lake processor. The piece emphasizes the immense complexity and continuous innovation required in core development, likening it to building and constantly improving the world's tallest tower.

feed.title: An Interview with Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger
feed.summary: This article features an interview with Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger, discussing his IDM 2.0 strategy, the acquisition of Tower Semiconductor, and Intel's role in the semiconductor industry. Gelsinger explains why Intel is committed to manufacturing its own chips despite the success of fabless competitors, emphasizing the geopolitical importance of a Western technology provider and the benefits of integrating foundry operations. He also addresses concerns about potential overcapacity in the industry and the future of x86 architecture.

feed.title: Behind Intel’s HPC Chip that Will Pierce the Exascale Barrier
feed.summary: Intel has unveiled new details about its Ponte Vecchio processor, which will power the Aurora supercomputer, aiming to achieve exascale performance. The chip utilizes advanced 2D and 3D chiplet integration technologies, combining over 100 billion transistors across 47 pieces of silicon using various manufacturing processes, including TSMC's N5 and Intel 7. This heterogeneous integration and sophisticated thermal management are crucial for achieving the targeted 2 exaflops for the Aurora supercomputer.

feed.title: UMC to Build New 22nm Wafer Fab in Singapore
feed.summary: United Microelectronics Corp. (UMC) announced plans to build a new advanced 22nm wafer fabrication plant in Singapore, investing $5 billion. The first phase of the new fab, which will be adjacent to its existing 300mm facility (Fab12i), is expected to commence production in late 2024 with a monthly capacity of 30,000 wafers. This expansion is supported by customer multi-year supply agreements, reflecting strong demand for UMC's 22/28nm technologies in sectors like 5G, IoT, and automotive, and aims to address the ongoing structural shortage in foundry capacity.

feed.title: Semiconductor sales to Russia banned, but that shouldn’t hurt Intel, AMD and other chip makers
feed.summary: Semiconductor companies are banning sales to Russia in response to its invasion of Ukraine. Despite a broader market sell-off and sanctions against Russia, an analyst believes these bans will have little financial impact on major chip makers like Intel and AMD because Russia's semiconductor market is likely too small to significantly affect their revenue.

feed.title: Use WGMI to Enter the Crypto Economy
feed.summary: The crypto economy is diversifying beyond direct Bitcoin ownership, with new ETFs like the Valkyrie Bitcoin Miners ETF (WGMI) offering access. WGMI focuses on bitcoin mining equities and is actively managed, allowing it to adapt to a nascent asset class with high growth potential. The ETF also incorporates a renewable energy overlay for its mining components, addressing environmental concerns associated with bitcoin mining.

feed.title: AMD, Arm, Google, Intel, TSMC and others join Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) group
feed.summary: Leading semiconductor companies including AMD, Arm, Google, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC have formed the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) industry consortium. This group aims to establish an open chiplet ecosystem by standardizing die-to-die interconnects, with the UCIe 1.0 specification already ratified. This initiative leverages existing PCI Express and Compute Express Link standards to enable easier mixing and matching of chiplet components from multiple vendors for System-on-Chip (SoC) construction.

feed.title: Leaders in Semiconductors, Packaging, IP Suppliers, Foundries, and Cloud Service Providers Join Forces to Standardize Chiplet Ecosystem
feed.summary: A consortium of leading technology companies, including ASE, AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC, has formed to standardize the chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) 1.0 specification, an open industry standard for die-to-die interconnects, aiming to enable multi-vendor compatibility for System-on-Chip construction. The consortium is open for membership and plans to develop future generations of the UCIe technology.

feed.title: Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) group combines AMD, Arm, Google, Intel, TSMC and more
feed.summary: The Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) group, formed by major tech companies like AMD, Arm, Google, Intel, and TSMC, has established a new industry consortium. Their goal is to standardize die-to-die interconnect guidelines and foster an open chiplet ecosystem, developing the UCIe 1.0 specification for interoperable multi-vendor chiplet components. This initiative aims to drive innovation in customizable package-level integration and address future challenges in semiconductor systems.

feed.title: DSP Concepts Launches TWS Toolkit Powered by Audio Weaver
feed.summary: DSP Concepts has launched a new toolkit for True Wireless Stereo (TWS) earbuds, powered by its Audio Weaver development platform. This toolkit features a reference design optimized for the Cadence Tensilica HiFi 5 DSP, includes algorithms from partners like Mimi Hearing Technologies and VisiSonics for personalized and spatial audio, and offers a Rapid Audio Prototyping Device (RAPID) TWS Kit to accelerate development. The initiative aims to help OEMs create advanced audio experiences for TWS products and reduce development risks.

feed.title: Nvidia hackers allegedly attempting to blackmail company into open-sourcing GPU drivers
feed.summary: The Lapsus$ ransomware gang, responsible for a recent attack on Nvidia, is allegedly attempting to blackmail the company into open-sourcing its GPU drivers. The attackers have threatened to leak sensitive GPU files if Nvidia does not comply with their demands by a set deadline, which also included a previous request to remove mining hashrate limiters. Nvidia has confirmed a cybersecurity incident where employee credentials and proprietary information were stolen, but denies ransomware deployment.

feed.title: Consortium formed to standardize chiplet ecosystem
feed.summary: A consortium comprising major tech companies like Intel, AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC has been formed to standardize the die-to-die interconnect and foster an open chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the UCIe 1.0 specification, which provides a ubiquitous interconnect standard for package-level integration, enabling easy mixing and matching of chiplet components from multiple vendors for System-on-Chip construction. The consortium aims to address customer demand for customizable package-level integration and reduce development time and cost through open standards.

feed.title: EPAM Shares Continue to Fall on Ukraine Crisis & Russia Exit
feed.summary: EPAM Systems' shares fell significantly due to the escalating Russia-Ukraine crisis and the company's decision to exit Russia. EPAM, with substantial exposure in Eastern Europe, announced it would discontinue services in Russia and offer humanitarian aid to Ukrainian employees, aligning with U.S. sanctions. The conflict is expected to impact the company's revenues and profitability due to its large workforce in the affected regions.

feed.title: Microchip Introduces Industry's Highest-Performance 16-Channel PCIe Gen 5 Enterprise NVMe SSD Controller
feed.summary: Microchip Technology Inc. has unveiled the Flashtec NVMe 4016 SSD controller, an industry-leading PCIe Gen 5 NVMe solution designed for high-performance enterprise and cloud-scale data centers. This 16-channel controller offers over 14 GB/s throughput and 3 million IOPS, supporting advanced features like PCIe link encryption, NVMe 2.0 compliance, and a new programmable Machine Learning engine. It aims to meet the escalating demands for bandwidth, low latency, and efficient resource utilization in modern data infrastructure.

feed.title: Apple unveils M1 Ultra, the world’s most powerful chip for a personal computer
feed.summary: Apple has announced M1 Ultra, its new chip for personal computers, which is available in the new Mac Studio. The M1 Ultra utilizes Apple's UltraFusion packaging architecture to combine two M1 Max chips, resulting in 114 billion transistors, a 20-core CPU, a 64-core GPU, and a 32-core Neural Engine. This powerful chip offers unprecedented performance and power efficiency for demanding tasks like 3D rendering and video editing, while consuming significantly less energy than high-end PC desktops.

feed.title: AMD Radeon™ PRO W6600X GPU for Mac Pro Unleashes New Creative Possibilities for Design and Content Creation Professionals
feed.summary: AMD has announced the availability of the new AMD Radeon™ PRO W6600X GPU for Mac Pro, aimed at creative professionals. Built on the AMD RDNA™ 2 architecture, it features AMD Infinity Cache™ and GDDR6 memory, offering high performance and visual fidelity for demanding professional applications. This GPU completes the AMD Radeon PRO W6000X series, providing a mid-range solution for content creation needs.

feed.title: Intel's $20 billion investment in Ohio promises thousands of jobs, but can the state and company deliver?
feed.summary: Intel's $20 billion investment in Ohio, set to create 3,000 permanent jobs and 7,000 construction jobs, faces scrutiny regarding its long-term success. While the state offers over $2 billion in incentives, experts question Intel's ability to maintain its competitive edge and Ohio's capacity to sustain the promised economic benefits, drawing comparisons to past manufacturing ventures in the state. Intel plans to address this by investing in talent development and implementing a new strategy to regain leadership in chip manufacturing.

feed.title: Industry Leaders Join Forces to Standardize Chiplet Ecosystem
feed.summary: A consortium of industry leaders, including AMD, Arm, Google Cloud, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, TSMC, and ASE, has formed to standardize die-to-die interconnects and foster an open chiplet ecosystem. They have ratified the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) 1.0 specification, an open industry standard for package-level connectivity. This initiative aims to reduce development time and cost, promoting customizable and interoperable chiplet designs for future silicon innovations.

feed.title: Micron CEO says chip shortage improving, but could last into 2023
feed.summary: Micron Technology CEO Sanjay Mehrotra stated that the semiconductor shortage, which has affected numerous industries, is improving but could extend into 2023. He highlighted the automotive sector as particularly impacted, with modern vehicles becoming "data centers on wheels" that require significant memory and storage. Mehrotra emphasized the need for a partnership between the U.S. government and the private sector to establish the U.S. as a leader in the chip industry, noting that Micron plans to invest over $150 billion globally in memory manufacturing, research, and development.

feed.title: TSMC’s largest customer makes up 26% of revenue
feed.summary: Sales from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co's (TSMC) largest customer, believed to be Apple Inc., accounted for 26% of its total revenue last year, reaching NT$405.4 billion. Its second-largest customer, suspected to be Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD), contributed 10% of sales. The US market remained TSMC's biggest buyer, while sales to China declined due to US sanctions against Huawei Technologies Co.

feed.title: University of Arizona Basketball Star Cate Reese Signs NIL Endorsement Deal with Newegg
feed.summary: Newegg, a global technology e-commerce retailer, announced its first NCAA athlete endorsement deal with University of Arizona women's basketball player Cate Reese. Reese, a three-time All-Pac-12 selection, will promote Newegg during the 2022 women’s NCAA basketball tournament as part of a national Name, Image, and Likeness (NIL) agreement. She highlights how technology is integral to balancing her demanding life as a student-athlete, using devices for academics, social connections, and even gaming.

feed.title: 2 Bullish Stocks in this Weak Market from The Professor!!!
feed.summary: In a weak market characterized by tech stock sell-offs due to inflation and interest rate concerns, "The Professor" highlights two bullish stocks: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) and Rambus Inc. (RMBS). JNJ is favored for its stability as a provider of essential goods, while RMBS is noted for its unusual relative strength in the semiconductor sector. Both stocks are presented with technical analysis setups, suggesting potential breakouts if the broader market stabilizes.

feed.title: AMD Launches the Ultimate Gaming Processor, Brings Enthusiast Performance to an Expanded Lineup of Ryzen Desktop Processors
feed.summary: AMD has announced the launch of its Ryzen 7 5800X3D processor, featuring groundbreaking 3D V-Cache technology that delivers up to 15% more gaming performance. Alongside this, AMD is expanding its Ryzen Desktop portfolio with six new "Zen 3" and "Zen 2" processors, offering more options for PC enthusiasts at various price points. The company also confirmed extended motherboard support for Ryzen 5000 Series processors on older AMD 300 Series chipsets.

feed.title: Aptos raises $200M to bring the Diem blockchain back to life
feed.summary: Aptos Labs, a startup founded by former employees of Meta's Diem blockchain project, has raised $200 million in strategic funding led by Andreessen Horowitz. The company aims to develop a safe and scalable blockchain network, learning from Diem's regulatory challenges. Aptos is building its own open-source blockchain for Web3 applications and expects to launch its public mainnet in the third quarter of 2022.

feed.title: Introducing AMD Software: Adrenalin Edition 2022 Release and AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 2.0 – Delivering High-Performance and Visually Stunning Gaming Experiences
feed.summary: AMD has announced the Adrenalin Edition 2022 software release and the new FidelityFX Super Resolution (FSR) 2.0 technology, aiming to enhance gaming performance and visual quality. The Adrenalin Edition includes Radeon Super Resolution (RSR) for driver-based upscaling compatible with thousands of games, and an updated AMD Link application for personal gaming clouds. FSR 2.0, expected in Q2 2022, is an advanced temporal upscaling technology that boosts framerates with high image quality, working across various AMD and competitor GPUs without needing dedicated machine learning hardware.

feed.title: When the world runs low on neon gas
feed.summary: The Russia-Ukraine war has significantly impacted the global supply of neon gas, which is crucial for semiconductor manufacturing. Ukraine, home to two major neon purification companies, has halted production due to the conflict, threatening to exacerbate the existing worldwide chip shortage. This situation highlights the precarity of specialized global supply chains, where the disruption of a single component can have far-reaching consequences for the technology industry, leading to higher prices, delivery delays, and potential product unavailability.

feed.title: Xiaomi re-brands Mi Fit to Zepp Life, Xiaomi Wear app to Mi Fitness
feed.summary: Xiaomi has rebranded its Mi Fit app to Zepp Life and the Xiaomi Wear app to Mi Fitness. This change reflects a growing unification of software between Xiaomi and Huami (Zepp Health), the manufacturer of Xiaomi's Mi Bands and Amazfit products. The updates are currently live on the Google Play Store, with iOS updates expected soon, and ensure no data loss for users.

feed.title: Is It Finally Time to Start Buying Intel Stock?
feed.summary: Intel, a pioneer in micro-processing, has seen its stock underperform competitors for years, but an ambitious makeover and new leadership may signal an attractive entry point for long-term investors. CEO Pat Gelsinger's plans for a massive $20 billion semiconductor plant in Ohio and other investments aim to restore Intel's market leadership. Despite investor skepticism reflected in its depressed stock price, Morningstar analysts see an appealing opportunity with a 27% discount to its fair value estimate and a 3.11% dividend yield.

feed.title: 3rd Gen AMD EPYC Processors with AMD 3D V-Cache Technology Deliver Outstanding Leadership Performance in Technical Computing Workloads
feed.summary: AMD has announced the general availability of its 3rd Gen EPYC processors with AMD 3D V-Cache technology, codenamed "Milan-X." These processors are the world's first data center CPUs to use 3D die stacking and are built on the "Zen 3" core architecture. They offer up to a 66% performance uplift in technical computing workloads and feature the industry's largest L3 cache for applications like CFD, FEA, and EDA.

feed.title: Keynote Wrap-Up: Turning Data Centers into ‘AI Factories,’ NVIDIA CEO Intros Hopper Architecture, H100 GPU, New Supercomputers, Software
feed.summary: During NVIDIA GTC, CEO Jensen Huang unveiled a vision of data centers as "AI Factories," introducing the new Hopper GPU architecture and H100 GPU, along with new AI and accelerated computing software. He also announced powerful new data-center-scale systems, including Grace CPU Superchip and DGX H100 supercomputers. Huang emphasized the role of Omniverse in integrating real and virtual worlds, speeding collaboration, and serving as a proving ground for new robotics and autonomous vehicles.

feed.title: NVIDIA Introduces Grace CPU Superchip
feed.summary: NVIDIA has unveiled its Grace CPU Superchip, an Arm Neoverse-based discrete data center CPU engineered for AI infrastructure and high-performance computing. This new chip boasts 144 high-performance cores and 1 terabyte/second memory, offering double the performance and energy-efficiency compared to current leading server chips. It is designed to work seamlessly with NVIDIA's existing software stacks and is expected to be available in the first half of 2023.

feed.title: NVIDIA Launches Omniverse for Developers: A Powerful and Collaborative Game Creation Environment
feed.summary: NVIDIA has launched new Omniverse features for developers, creating a powerful and collaborative environment for game creation. This update streamlines game development by integrating AI and NVIDIA RTX tools, enabling real-time collaboration and enhancing character animation and asset management. Key new features include Omniverse Audio2Face, Omniverse Nucleus Cloud, Omniverse DeepSearch, and an Unreal Engine 5 Omniverse Connector.

feed.title: This tech group is leading the charge in sustainable Web 3.0 development
feed.summary: Atlas Technology Management, a Singapore-headquartered technology group, is expanding its Web 3.0 computing infrastructure and committing to sustainable development. The company aims to achieve 100% green energy adoption by 2024 and plans to expand its global coverage to over 30 countries in the next five years. Atlas is also exploring advanced technologies like underwater data centers to minimize environmental impact.

feed.title: 10 Servers With AMD EPYC Milan-X New Server CPUs
feed.summary: AMD has launched its third-generation EPYC processors with 3-D V-Cache technology, code-named Milan-X, which is the world's first data center CPU using 3-D die stacking. This innovation allows for 768 MB of L3 cache, significantly enhancing performance for technical computing workloads like FEA, EDA, and CFD. Major server vendors such as Dell Technologies, HPE, Cisco, Lenovo, and Supermicro are rolling out new or upgraded servers supporting these processors, aiming to offer improved performance, lower total cost of ownership, and reduced power consumption.

feed.title: Tackling Global Supply Chain Disruption
feed.summary: This article discusses how Microsoft has addressed global supply chain disruptions caused by the pandemic, focusing on leveraging technology for agility and resilience. It highlights digitizing factories, using AI/machine learning for data and logistics, and integrating sustainability. The article also introduces Microsoft's Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Insights as a solution for organizations seeking to build resilient supply chains.

feed.title: Possible Nvidia, Intel GPU Alliance Could Frustrate Rivals AMD, TSMC
feed.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is in talks with Intel to use its foundries for GPU production, a move that could significantly impact competitors AMD and TSMC. This potential alliance would diversify Nvidia's supply base and challenge TSMC's dominance in the foundry market, while also putting pressure on AMD's supercomputing ambitions. Both Intel and Nvidia are interested in the collaboration, with Intel aiming to become the top GPU foundry by 2025.

feed.title: Nvidia considers teaming up with Intel on chip manufacturing
feed.summary: Nvidia is in discussions with Intel about potentially using Intel as a foundry for manufacturing its chips. This consideration comes amid a global semiconductor chip shortage and Intel's efforts to become a major U.S. foundry supplier, despite the challenges of aligning technologies and business models between the companies. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang expressed openness to the idea, noting existing close collaboration between the firms.

feed.title: The Top 5: Biggest Automotive Semiconductor Manufacturers
feed.summary: The automotive semiconductor market, valued at $43.6 billion in 2021, is projected to exceed $115 billion by 2030, driven by the growth of electric vehicles and autonomous technologies. This article identifies the top five automotive semiconductor manufacturers, excluding TSMC which dominates the foundry market, and details their market share, financial performance, and strategic investments. The leading companies are Infineon Technologies AG, NXP Semiconductors N.V., Renesas Electronics Corp., Texas Instruments, Inc., and STMicroelectronics N.V.
